summary: Good audience recording with clear instrumentation and a strong Brent‑focused set highlighted by Roses and Louie Louie.
review: The audience tape captured on shotgun mics is surprisingly clean for a 1989 AUD, delivering audible vocals and crisp keyboards while missing a little low‑end bass. The show itself is generally praised – Brent Mydland’s fiery organ work, Jerry’s vocal charm on Roses and a memorable Louie Louie dominate the positives, though some listeners note a lazy first‑set Walkin’ Blues and an uneven second set.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (13 total) give 3‑5 stars, praising both performance and tape quality; only a few note flaws. Weighted toward show quality (70%) the rating lands just under 4.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Shotgun mics on Nak 300s give clear separation of instruments and vocals; crowd bleed is low but bass lacks some punch. No major drop‑outs, though one reviewer called it "terrible" – overall the tape is listenable and better than many SBD copies.

poor_songs: Louie Louie (worst version for some listeners)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens with mixed energy – a solid Roses and Desolation Row but a lackluster Walkin' Blues. Brent’s keyboards lift the mood, especially on Foolish Heart. The second set builds momentum with an over‑the‑top Louie Louie and strong organ solos, though a few listeners felt the later portion sagged.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals praised on Roses and generally fine throughout; some note early‑set energy dip.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives glowing remarks – described as "atomic", delivering fiery solos on Foolish Heart, Big Liver/Big River and Louie Louie.
